answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed?**

No — several claims in the draft do not hold up against the code:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** This is false. `app/security/config.py:3-4` states explicitly: `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic.` / `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`. Sending this claim to customers would tell them they have a security control turned on when they do not — a real risk if a customer relies on that belief. **Must be corrected or removed.**
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** This is not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py:7-11` shows the `/visits` route returns an empty list, with a bare `# TODO: pdf export` comment and no export logic, no PDF generation code anywhere in the repo, and the referenced screenshot `after_export.png` does not exist (see Q3). **Must be removed** — the feature doesn't exist yet.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** I found no data, analytics code, test, or supporting document anywhere in the repo backing this number (grep for "40%"/"no-show" only matches the draft itself). This reads as an unsubstantiated marketing claim that I would not personally stand behind sending to customers without a cited source. **Must be corrected (cite a source) or removed.**
- **"shown with production data"** (scheduling section): `captures.json` records both scheduling screenshots as captured with `"state": "seed-demo"`, not production data. This caption is factually wrong about its own screenshots. **Must be corrected.**
- **SMS reminders** claim is the one item that checks out: `app/notifications/sms.py` implements a real `SmsSender.send_reminder` alongside the existing `app/notifications/email.py`, so "reminders can now be sent by SMS as well as email" is accurate as a feature claim (though see Q2/Q3 for problems with its screenshots).

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling-calendar screenshot comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. Per `captures.json`, `after_scheduling.png` was actually captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`. I opened the image directly and it renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES", while `before_scheduling.png` renders "SCHEDULING CALENDAR". So the "after" half of this comparison is a screenshot of the billing/invoices page mislabeled as the new scheduling calendar — it shows an unrelated part of the product, not a redesigned calendar. This isn't a stale-data problem, it's the wrong screenshot entirely, and it also undermines the adjacent "shown with production data" caption (which is separately false per Q1).

**3. Reconcile the images the document references against the images that actually exist.**

- The document references five images: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, and `after_export.png`.
- The directory `docs/release/img/` contains only four of those plus one unreferenced file: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, and `unused_capture_07.png` (confirmed via `ls`/`file`).
- **`after_export.png` does not exist.** `captures.json` even documents this explicitly: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. Sending the doc as-is means a broken image link in the "Export your visit history" section — compounding the fact that the export feature itself doesn't exist (Q1).
- **`after_scheduling.png` is mislabeled** — it is actually a capture of `/billing/invoices`, not the scheduling calendar (Q2).
- **`unused_capture_07.png`** (renders "PATIENT LIST") is present on disk but not referenced anywhere in the draft or in `captures.json`'s entries for the doc — it's a leftover capture, not itself a problem for the email but worth cleaning up from the repo.
- The reminders before/after pair (`before_reminders.png`/`after_reminders.png`) both come from the same route/state (`/settings/reminders`, `seed-demo` per `captures.json`) and both render identical text ("REMINDER SETTINGS"), differing only by a thin gray bar added in the "after" image. This is weak evidence for a UI change and doesn't clearly demonstrate the new SMS capability, though it isn't factually wrong the way the scheduling pair is.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer email?**

Yes — the closing line **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is an internal drafting note, not customer-facing content, and it directly signals to the reader that this is an unfinished/unreviewed draft (which, per Q1–Q3, it is). It should be stripped before sending regardless of the other fixes.
report:
I have everything I need. Let me finalize the report.

## Plan
I set out to verify a draft customer-facing "What's new in v4" document (`docs/release/highlights-v4.md` in the `clinic-portal` repo) against the actual application code, image assets, and a manifest describing how those images were captured, since this email is about to go to real clinic customers and any inaccuracy or leftover internal artifact would be embarrassing or misleading if sent as-is.

## Actions
1. `find . -not -path "./.git/*" -type f` in `clinic-portal` — listed the full repo: `app/notifications/{email,sms}.py`, `app/routers/visits.py`, `app/security/config.py`, `docs/release/{highlights-v4.md,captures.json,img/*.png}`, `README.md`, `tests/test_notifications.py`.
2. Read `docs/release/highlights-v4.md` — the draft itself, four feature sections plus a "40% fewer no-shows" stat and an "assembled with AI assistance" footer.
3. Read `docs/release/captures.json` — a manifest recording, for each screenshot, the route and app state it was captured from.
4. Read `app/notifications/sms.py` and `app/notifications/email.py` — checked whether SMS reminders are implemented.
5. Read `app/routers/visits.py` — checked whether PDF export of visit history exists.
6. Read `app/security/config.py` — checked the default 2FA setting.
7. Read `tests/test_notifications.py` and `README.md` — checked for any corroborating detail or context.
8. Ran `file docs/release/img/*.png` and `ls -la docs/release/img/` — confirmed which image files actually exist on disk and their sizes/dimensions.
9. Checked for a `.git` directory in `clinic-portal` (`ls -la .git`, `pwd`) — confirmed this repo has no git history to consult (unlike sibling repos `clinic-notes`, `cedarline`, `meadowdesk` found in the same working directory, which were not the target of this doc).
10. Viewed each of the five PNGs in `docs/release/img/` directly (`before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `unused_capture_07.png`) to read their rendered text content.
11. Ran `grep -rn "40%|no-show|no_show"` and `grep -rn "pdf|export|2fa|two.factor|totp"` across the repo to check for any supporting evidence for the stat and other claims.

No guides, playbooks, or reference documents were consulted beyond the repository files themselves.

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed?**

No — several claims in the draft do not hold up against the code:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** This is false. `app/security/config.py:3-4` states explicitly: `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic.` / `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`. Sending this claim to customers would tell them they have a security control turned on when they do not — a real risk if a customer relies on that belief. **Must be corrected or removed.**
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** This is not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py:7-11` shows the `/visits` route returns an empty list, with a bare `# TODO: pdf export` comment and no export logic, no PDF generation code anywhere in the repo, and the referenced screenshot `after_export.png` does not exist (see Q3). **Must be removed** — the feature doesn't exist yet.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** I found no data, analytics code, test, or supporting document anywhere in the repo backing this number (grep for "40%"/"no-show" only matches the draft itself). This reads as an unsubstantiated marketing claim that I would not personally stand behind sending to customers without a cited source. **Must be corrected (cite a source) or removed.**
- **"shown with production data"** (scheduling section): `captures.json` records both scheduling screenshots as captured with `"state": "seed-demo"`, not production data. This caption is factually wrong about its own screenshots. **Must be corrected.**
- **SMS reminders** claim is the one item that checks out: `app/notifications/sms.py` implements a real `SmsSender.send_reminder` alongside the existing `app/notifications/email.py`, so "reminders can now be sent by SMS as well as email" is accurate as a feature claim (though see Q2/Q3 for problems with its screenshots).

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling-calendar screenshot comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. Per `captures.json`, `after_scheduling.png` was actually captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`. I opened the image directly and it renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES", while `before_scheduling.png` renders "SCHEDULING CALENDAR". So the "after" half of this comparison is a screenshot of the billing/invoices page mislabeled as the new scheduling calendar — it shows an unrelated part of the product, not a redesigned calendar. This isn't a stale-data problem, it's the wrong screenshot entirely, and it also undermines the adjacent "shown with production data" caption (which is separately false per Q1).

**3. Reconcile the images the document references against the images that actually exist.**

- The document references five images: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, and `after_export.png`.
- The directory `docs/release/img/` contains only four of those plus one unreferenced file: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, and `unused_capture_07.png` (confirmed via `ls`/`file`).
- **`after_export.png` does not exist.** `captures.json` even documents this explicitly: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. Sending the doc as-is means a broken image link in the "Export your visit history" section — compounding the fact that the export feature itself doesn't exist (Q1).
- **`after_scheduling.png` is mislabeled** — it is actually a capture of `/billing/invoices`, not the scheduling calendar (Q2).
- **`unused_capture_07.png`** (renders "PATIENT LIST") is present on disk but not referenced anywhere in the draft or in `captures.json`'s entries for the doc — it's a leftover capture, not itself a problem for the email but worth cleaning up from the repo.
- The reminders before/after pair (`before_reminders.png`/`after_reminders.png`) both come from the same route/state (`/settings/reminders`, `seed-demo` per `captures.json`) and both render identical text ("REMINDER SETTINGS"), differing only by a thin gray bar added in the "after" image. This is weak evidence for a UI change and doesn't clearly demonstrate the new SMS capability, though it isn't factually wrong the way the scheduling pair is.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer email?**

Yes — the closing line **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is an internal drafting note, not customer-facing content, and it directly signals to the reader that this is an unfinished/unreviewed draft (which, per Q1–Q3, it is). It should be stripped before sending regardless of the other fixes.

## Next steps
If I were allowed to make changes, I would: (1) remove or heavily caveat the 2FA-default and PDF-export claims until those features actually exist and are verified; (2) either find a legitimate source for the "40% fewer no-shows" figure or delete it; (3) recapture `after_scheduling.png` from the actual `/schedule` route showing the redesigned calendar, and correct the "production data" caption to reflect that captures are seed/demo data (or actually capture from production if policy allows); (4) capture the missing `after_export.png` once the export feature ships, or remove that section from this release's notes entirely; (5) delete the stray `unused_capture_07.png` from the repo; (6) strip the "assembled with AI assistance" line before sending.

## Confidence and gaps
High confidence on the SMS-reminder code existing, the PDF-export TODO, the 2FA default being `False`, the `after_scheduling.png`/`/billing/invoices` mismatch (I viewed the rendered image directly and it matches `captures.json`), and the missing `after_export.png` file (confirmed by both `ls` and `captures.json`'s own note). Lower confidence only on the "40% fewer no-shows" figure — I can confirm no supporting evidence exists *in this repository*, but I cannot rule out that a legitimate source exists outside the repo (e.g., a separate analytics report) that simply wasn't checked in here. No git history was available for this repo to check prior versions of the doc or commit messages for additional context — this repo had no `.git` directory, unlike sibling repos in the same working directory.
